page: Amazon Web Services r8g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Cache resources include 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. No GPUs are included.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaches 189.8 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. Single-core processing is also robust, with Geekbench single-core scores and single-threaded compression/decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations, which average 17.56 million operations per second, fall within the middle 50% range.

Tradeoffs are evident in cryptographic and complex AI workloads. The Passmark CPU encryption test scores in the bottom 25%, and LLM inference tasks show limitations, with prompt processing for medium models falling into the bottom 10%. Given these performance characteristics and the high memory-to-CPU ratio, the r8g.2xlarge is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads such as caching layers, database operations, in-memory analytics, and text processing, while being less optimal for heavy cryptographic processing or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8g.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 36 MB of L3 cache. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance includes a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps and lacks local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and large-block read bandwidth in the top 25%. However, cryptographic performance and medium-model LLM inference represent notable tradeoffs, scoring in the lower percentiles. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, including database operations, caching layers, and text processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8g.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth performance for database and caching workloads.
